title: Nucleus Facade Response Envelope
description: Wrapper shape returned by every facade tool dispatch when NUCLEUS_ENVELOPE != 'off'. Consumers pin to schema_version and regression-test against this artifact.

ok.description: Success flag. False indicates error was encountered; error field SHOULD be populated.

data.description: Handler payload. Any JSON-serializable value (object, array, string, number, boolean, null).

brain_id.description: Identifier of the brain that served this response. Resolves from .brain/manifest.yaml or falls back to 'unknown'.

session_id.description: MCP session identifier. Falls back to process-scoped id (proc-<hex>) if session-level id not available.

schema_version.description: Envelope schema version. Bumped on breaking contract changes.

warnings.description: Non-fatal warnings (e.g. constitution policy advisories, deprecations). ok MAY still be true.

error.description: Structured error block when ok=false; null otherwise.

error_type.description: Short classification token. Recommended values: transport_closed, handler_error, timeout, validation_error, not_found, forbidden, rate_limited, internal_error.

recovery_hint.description: Actionable one-liner for the user or automation (e.g. 'nucleus restart --force').

last_healthy_at.description: ISO8601 timestamp of last known healthy state. Used by transport_closed.

pid.description: Process id of the server that encountered the error.

inflight_writes.description: Identifiers of writes that were in-flight at failure time.
